content.full_text:
Tina Post 
Technical Assistance 
(916) 322.5662 
Deputy City Attorney 
450 North Crescent Drive 
Beverly Hills, CA 
90210 
Dear Ms. Post: 
Administration 
322·5660 
Executive/legal 
322·5901 
August 22, 1984 
Enforcement 
322-6,(,(1 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice, 
Our Advice No. A-84-214 
This is to confirm our conversation of last Friday and to 
pose some follow-up questions. 
First, you have confirmed that 
Councilmember Brown's hotel business, located at 8400 Sunset 
Boulevard in west Hollywood, is named the Sunset Plaza Hotel. 
My information reflects that it is a Best western hotel which is 
also listed by AAA as a 3-Diamond establishment. 
Second, you have inquired of Councilmember Brown with 
respect to Equity Hotel Supply, a partnership listed on 
Schedule A of his 1983 Statement of Economic Interests. 
You 
advised me that he told you that the partnership, which was set 
up as a co-op, has been dissolved. 
While in existence, it only 
served the Sunset Plaza Hotel and other hotels owned by members 
of the partnership owning Sunset Plaza Hotel. 
I have the following series of questions, which are based 
upon the Commission's rulings in the Oglesby Opinion and the 
Nord Opinion (copies enclosed). 
The questions derive from my 
review of Councilmember Brown's Statement and our conversations. 
1. 
What is the name of the business entity (partnership) 
which operates Sunset Plaza Hotel and in which Councilmember 
Brown is a general partner (5%) and a limited partner (7-1/2%)? 
Is it Forman & Brown Development Company? 
That business entity 
appears to be a corporation instaed of a partnership. 
Is he a 
10% or greater owner in either Equity Hotel Supply or Forman & 
Brown Development Company? 
2. 
What is the use on the property located at 281 South 
Robertson Blvd., Beverly Hills? 
Is he a 10% or greater owner of 
this property? 
Is the property rented out, and if so, to whom? 
If not rented out, to what use is he putting it? 
.. .... 


Tina Post 
August 22, 1984 
Page 2 
3. 
The same series of questions apply to the 8320 Melrose 
Avenue property and to the 1639 South LaCienega Boulevard 
property. 
4. 
How many general partners are there in the Sunset Plaza 
Hotel business entity? 
Is anyone of them a 50% or more general 
partner or a controlling general partner? 
Depending on the answers to the foregoing questions, I may 
or may not need additional information. 
Your prompt responses 
will facilitate my analysis of Councilmember Brown's 
circumstances. 
REL: km 
Sincerely, 
r~Lf'f8.J L 
Robert E. Leldigh 
~7 
Counsel, Legal Divi/ion 
.......

sections.question_synthetic: The FPPC counsel seeks clarification regarding Councilmember Brown's financial interests, including the structure and ownership percentages of business entities operating the Sunset Plaza Hotel and other properties, and whether he meets the 10% or greater ownership threshold for disclosure purposes.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a follow-up correspondence/inquiry letter from FPPC counsel to city attorney, not a traditional advice letter response. No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections present. Document contains multiple detailed questions rather than advice. Formatted as letter confirming prior conversation and requesting additional information.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: The FPPC counsel seeks clarification regarding Councilmember Brown's financial interests, including the structure and ownership percentages of business entities operating the Sunset Plaza Hotel and other properties, and whether he meets the 10% or greater ownership threshold for disclosure purposes.

embedding.summary: FPPC counsel Robert E. Leldigh writes to Deputy City Attorney Tina Post following up on a prior conversation regarding Councilmember Brown's economic interests, posing four series of questions about the business entities owning the Sunset Plaza Hotel, three Beverly Hills properties, and partnership structure to determine disclosure obligations under FPPC regulations.
